commands.author: Ittiz

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there was no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- Security follow-up: Plugin UI entry registration, launch/session auth, and iframe bridge proxying now validate canonical decoded plugin roots, so encoded dot-segment paths cannot let one plugin UI session authorize sibling plugin routes.
- Scope boundary: This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. The proof plugin lives separately on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but before this PR they had no generic, first-class way to place a Control UI navigation entry that launches a plugin-owned UI through the gateway authorization boundary. This PR adds that contract while keeping plugin entry paths and iframe bridge requests confined to the owning plugin root.

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `b8e35876a18a36390c867080e475d53f26fb4e15`.
- Behavior addressed: Plugin UI entry points can be listed/launched/rendered; sandboxed opaque-origin iframes receive the MessagePort bridge; decoded/canonicalized plugin paths remain confined to the owning plugin root; new entry-point response schemas are exported through the public protocol barrel.
- Real environment tested: rebuilt exact-head local Linux checkout, throwaway loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:19888`, temporary plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths`, Control UI driven in a real headless Chromium browser, plus direct HTTP route checks.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
node .artifacts/pr80388-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live browser/plugin-route evidence from the throwaway exact-head gateway:

```json
{
  "head": "OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (726f348)",
  "controlUiUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:19888/overview#token=REDACTED",
  "pluginEntryVisible": true,
  "iframeMarker": "PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_IFRAME_READY",
  "bridgeText": "BRIDGE_STATUS: 200 {\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}",
  "directUnauthStatus": 401,
  "directAuthStatus": 200,
  "directAuthBody": "{\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}"
}
```

Focused test evidence:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.24s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  68 passed (68)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 68.19s

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0

$ OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
[build-all] phase timings: total 175.6s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts` verifies sandboxed in-app plugin requests are proxied only for plugin-owned paths and stale iframe bridge ports cannot service current-frame requests.
- `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts` rejects Plugin UI Entry Point sessions when decoded path candidates leave the plugin root.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` rejects encoded dot-segment sibling escapes at entry-point registration.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` rejects external, contextual, cross-plugin, and encoded sibling paths.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes. Plugins can expose Control UI entry points.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Entry-point launch uses short-lived Plugin UI launch/session tokens.
- Data access scope changed? Yes. Entry points declare required operator scopes.
- Mitigation: Entry-point paths are constrained to canonical plugin-owned roots; launch/session auth and iframe proxying reject decoded sibling escapes; direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

commands.author: hyjkimbot

target.title: docs(telegram): clarify account-local group config
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram docs pointed group setup at root \`channels.telegram.groups\`, which is easy to misread in multi-account configs.
- Why it matters: a bot account can receive a group message from Telegram but still drop it during OpenClaw group policy/auth because the active account has no account-local \`groups\` rule.
- What changed: documented account-local Telegram group rules for multi-account setups and added a non-mutating doctor warning for root groups plus enabled allowlisted accounts missing \`accounts.<id>.groups\`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): runtime authorization and group inheritance behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: A multi-account Telegram setup had a root \`channels.telegram.groups\` rule for a group, but the active bot account lacked \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`. A bot-authored group mention was delivered to the gateway but dropped before agent dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway with multiple Telegram bot accounts and a private Telegram group used for bot-to-bot coordination. Identifiers are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the local working configuration by copying the group rule into \`channels.telegram.accounts.default.groups["<GROUP_CHAT_ID>"]\`, restarting the installed gateway, and sending a bot-authored \`REQUEST @<bot> ...\` group message from a second trusted bot account.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied redacted runtime log output from the local gateway after the account-local group rule was added:

\`\`\`text
Inbound message telegram:group:<GROUP_CHAT_ID> -> @<BOT_USERNAME> (group, 168 chars)
⇄ res ✓ message.action 310ms channel=telegram conn=<REDACTED> id=<REDACTED>
\`\`\`

- Observed result after fix: Account-local Telegram group config fixed the real setup without a runtime code change. This PR documents that path and adds a doctor warning so similar configs are easier to diagnose.
- What was not tested: I did not deploy this branch as a packaged OpenClaw release. The runtime behavior is unchanged; the new doctor warning is covered by focused tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Copied redacted runtime log output from the failing trial before the account-local group rule existed:

\`\`\`text
[telegram][diag] spooled update <UPDATE_ID> completed without agent dispatch kind=message chat=<GROUP_CHAT_ID> chatType=group message=<MESSAGE_ID> from=<TRUSTED_BOT_USER_ID> fromBot=true fromUsername=<TRUSTED_BOT_USERNAME> text="REQUEST @<BOT_USERNAME> <test-message>" outcome=dropped reason=group-policy-or-auth
\`\`\`

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The docs emphasized root \`channels.telegram.groups\` for group setup, while multi-account users need to reason about the active account's group rules.
- Missing detection / guardrail: \`openclaw doctor\` did not flag a config where root Telegram groups existed but enabled allowlisted accounts had no account-local group rules.
- Contributing context (if known): Existing troubleshooting guidance made Telegram privacy/raw delivery and group policy/auth look similar until a diagnostic identified \`group-policy-or-auth\`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: \`extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts\`
- Scenario the test should lock in: root \`channels.telegram.groups\` exists in a multi-account Telegram config, and an enabled allowlisted account has no \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the changed behavior is a non-mutating doctor warning, so the focused doctor scanner/formatter tests cover the new surface directly.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing Telegram doctor tests covered invalid sender IDs, apiRoot warnings, and first-time group guidance, but not this multi-account root-groups trap.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

\`openclaw doctor\` can now warn about multi-account Telegram configs where root group rules exist but enabled allowlisted accounts have no account-local group rules. Telegram docs now show the account-local group config path for multi-account setups.

## Diagram (if applicable)

\`\`\`text
Before:
multi-account Telegram config -> root channels.telegram.groups only -> group update can be dropped by account policy/auth

After:
multi-account Telegram config -> channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups -> account has explicit group authorization
\`\`\`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any \`Yes\`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw source checkout for tests; installed OpenClaw gateway for real behavior proof
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted):

\`\`\`json5
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      accounts: {
        default: {
          groups: {
            "<GROUP_CHAT_ID>": {
              requireMention: true,
              allowFrom: ["<OWNER_USER_ID>", "<TRUSTED_BOT_USER_ID>"],
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
\`\`\`

### Steps

1. Configure multiple Telegram bot accounts.
2. Put a group rule only under root \`channels.telegram.groups\`.
3. Send a trusted bot-authored group mention to the active bot account.
4. Move/copy the group rule to \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`.
5. Restart the gateway and resend the trusted bot-authored group mention.

### Expected

- Docs make the account-local group path clear for multi-account configs.
- Doctor warns when root groups exist but enabled allowlisted accounts are missing account-local group rules.
- A correctly configured active account receives the trusted group mention as a normal agent turn.

### Actual

- Before the local config fix, the group message was raw-delivered but dropped during group policy/auth.
- After account-local group config, the trusted bot-authored group mention dispatched normally.
- With this PR, focused tests confirm the new doctor warning and skip cases.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local real setup with multi-account Telegram bots; root group rule did not authorize the active account, account-local group rule did.
- Edge cases checked: disabled accounts, \`groupPolicy: "open"\` accounts, mixed open plus allowlisted accounts, single-account configs.
- What you did **not** verify: full packaged release build or live doctor command from an installed package containing this patch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The doctor warning could be noisy for accounts intentionally configured without group allowlists.
  - Mitigation: The warning skips disabled accounts and accounts whose effective \`groupPolicy\` is \`open\` or \`disabled\`, and tests cover those cases.

target.title: fix(agents): return updatedInput instead of behavior:allow for Claude Code 2.1.x PermissionResult schema (fixes #95171)
target.body:
## Summary

Claude Code `2.1.x` tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. The new union accepts `{ updatedInput: <record> }` for allow decisions instead of the older `{ behavior: "allow" }` shape. OpenClaw's bridge still returned the old shape, causing every tool call through the permission/approval flow to fail with a `ZodError` on Claude Code ≥ 2.1.

This PR updates both permission response sites to return `{ updatedInput: input }` for allow decisions, matching the new schema. Deny responses (`{ behavior: "deny", message }`) are unchanged.

## Changes

- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` — live session `can_use_tool` control response: `{ behavior: "allow" }` → `{ updatedInput: request.input ?? {} }`
- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` — hook relay `PermissionRequest` decision: `{ behavior: "allow" }` → `{ updatedInput: input ?? {} }`; added `input` parameter to `renderPermissionDecisionResponse` signature; updated 3 allow call sites to pass `request.toolInput`
- `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` — updated 3 allow assertions to check `updatedInput` with expected tool input
- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` — updated 9 allow assertions to check `updatedInput`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `canUseTool` permission response returns `{ updatedInput }` instead of deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }` for Claude Code 2.1.x compatibility
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node v24, openclaw upstream/main @ da03996a
- **Steps run after the patch:** Ran the auto-reply verification suite for both changed test files
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n 'updatedInput' src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts
src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts:878:            updatedInput:
src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts:397:              ? { updatedInput: input ?? {} }

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const s1=fs.readFileSync('src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts','utf8'); const s2=fs.readFileSync('src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts','utf8'); console.log('Old behavior:allow removed:', !s1.includes('behavior: \"allow\"') && !s2.includes('behavior: \"allow\"')); console.log('New updatedInput present:', s1.includes('updatedInput') && s2.includes('updatedInput: input'));"
Old behavior:allow removed: true
New updatedInput present: true

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts
 60 passed (60)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
 75 passed (75)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All 135 tests pass. Both source files use `updatedInput` for allow decisions; old `behavior: "allow"` pattern fully removed.
- **What was not tested:** Live Claude Code 2.1.x session (requires real Claude Code CLI installation and runtime)

target.title: fix: keep text transform runtime imports hashed
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a released-package gateway crash-loop path where new chunks can import expanded text-transform exports through a stable alias that an already-running gateway may have cached from an older package.
- Keeps new `text-transforms.runtime` imports on the hashed runtime chunk while still generating the stable wrapper for old chunks.
- Adds legacy compat wrappers for the shipped v2026.5.22 and v2026.6.8 text-transform runtime hashes.
- Out of scope: changing provider runtime behavior or plugin text-transform semantics.

## Linked context

Closes #95057

Related #95057

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested in maintainer workflow to repair the P1 release regression.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: macOS LaunchAgent-managed gateway can crash-loop after upgrading when new provider/CLI chunks import `n/i/a/r/t` from `./text-transforms.runtime.js` but the live process has an older stable wrapper cached that only exposed `t`.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout on macOS with Node 22 toolchain and npm package inspection of published `openclaw@2026.5.22` and `openclaw@2026.6.8`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs` after a source build had produced `dist`; then inspected generated importers with `rg -n "text-transforms\.runtime\.js|text-transforms\.runtime-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.js" dist/provider-runtime*.js dist/cli-backends*.js dist/prepare.runtime*.js dist/execute.runtime*.js dist/selection*.js dist/compact*.js` and inspected `dist/text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js` plus `dist/text-transforms.runtime-sEqsN4pN.js`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
--- runtime-postbuild stderr ---
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime imports completed in 5858ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime aliases completed in 572ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy root runtime compat aliases completed in 1ms
--- importers ---
dist/cli-backends-uScbGHoe.js:4:import { r as mergePluginTextTransforms, t as resolveRuntimeTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/provider-runtime-Cqktwbuy.js:10:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements, r as mergePluginTextTransforms, t as resolveRuntimeTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/compact-pRQItHot.js:12:import { i as wrapStreamFnTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/execute.runtime-DCIHvX5t.js:5:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/prepare.runtime-Db3n21Sv.js:11:import { n as applyPluginTextReplacements } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
dist/selection-DS_psmoB.js:34:import { a as createStreamIteratorWrapper, i as wrapStreamFnTextTransforms } from "./text-transforms.runtime-DhZfO7FJ.js";
--- compat ---
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime.js";
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime.js";
```

- Observed result after fix: new chunks no longer depend on the stable `text-transforms.runtime.js` module namespace for expanded exports, while old shipped hashed runtime names still resolve through compat wrappers.
- What was not tested: a full macOS Apple Silicon LaunchAgent upgrade from `openclaw@2026.5.22` to this unpublished branch package.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm build` completed `tsdown` and `runtime-postbuild` but timed out at 15 minutes during the later plugin SDK d.ts stage, so the package-level proof is based on the completed generated `dist` runtime-postbuild output plus focused tests rather than a full build command exit 0.
- Before evidence:

```text
2026.5.22 dist/text-transforms.runtime.js:
export * from "./text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js";

2026.5.22 dist/text-transforms.runtime-D9-SpAmI.js export surface:
export { resolveRuntimeTextTransforms as t };

2026.6.8 importer examples:
dist/provider-runtime-Cp9BfFXg.js imports n/r/t from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
dist/cli-backends-f9hsjoJk.js imports r/t from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
dist/selection-kQiC501t.js imports a/i from "./text-transforms.runtime.js"
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/runtime-postbuild.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs test/scripts/runtime-postbuild.test.ts`
- `node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added runtime-postbuild tests proving text-transform runtime importers stay hashed after the stable alias export surface expansion.
- Added runtime-postbuild tests proving previous published text-transform runtime hash names get compat wrappers.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The package comparison shows the old stable wrapper exposed only `t`, while the new released chunks import more aliases through the same stable module path. That can fail in a live process with a cached older wrapper namespace.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. It changes generated package runtime import paths to avoid upgrade-time gateway crashes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Runtime package postbuild aliasing for live upgrades.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is scoped to one runtime alias skip and two shipped hash compat entries, with focused fixture tests and generated `dist` proof that old compat wrappers and new hashed importers coexist.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for CI and ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer/CI may still choose to run full package acceptance or a macOS LaunchAgent package-upgrade proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Real behavior proof body was updated with copied postbuild output after the initial gate did not recognize the evidence line as copied live output.

target.title:
fix(telegram): convert newlines to
in rich HTML for paragraph breaks
target.body:
## Summary Telegram's `sendRichMessage` API (Bot API 10.1) parses HTML properly, treating literal `\n` as whitespace. This collapses paragraph breaks in plain text — multi-paragraph messages render as a single block with no visual separation. ### Root cause `markdownToTelegramRichHtml` renders Markdown IR to HTML via `renderMarkdownWithMarkers`, which uses `escapeHtml` as the text escape function. This preserves `\n` characters literally in the output HTML. For the standard `sendMessage` path (`parse_mode: "HTML"`), Telegram treats `\n` as a line break — so this works fine. But for `sendRichMessage` (`{ html: "..." }`), `\n` is parsed as whitespace and collapsed. ### Fix Add `convertNewlinesToBrsForRichHtml()` to the rich HTML pipeline that: - Converts `\n` to `
` in plain text and between inline tags (e.g. `\n\n`) - Preserves `\n` inside `
`, ``, and `` (whitespace is semantically significant)
- Preserves structural whitespace between block-level/container tags (e.g. `\n`, `\n
`) to avoid inserting invalid `
` children inside containers

### Test plan

- [x] All 55 `format.test.ts` tests pass (3 existing tests updated, 3 new tests added)
- [x] All 2160 telegram extension tests pass (`pnpm vitest run extensions/telegram/src/`)
- [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `codex review --base origin/main` — clean (no accepted/actionable findings)

### Real behavior proof

**Setup:** OpenClaw v2026.6.9-beta.1, Telegram DM, `channels.telegram.richMessages: true`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "partial"`.

**Before fix (observed by user):** Multi-paragraph assistant replies render as a single block in Telegram — paragraph breaks (`\n\n`) are collapsed, all text runs together:

```
搞定，改了 3 个文件： AGENTS.md (Cha) — 删掉了 Discord/WhatsApp/Telegram 三条限制，替换为一条 Telegram rich text 说明 AGENTS.md (WoCha) — 同上 TOOLS.md (Cha) md2pdf 章节 — 去掉了表格相关触发条件： • "Why it exists" 去掉 GFM tables ...
```

**After fix:** Applied the patch to the running gateway. Multi-paragraph replies now render with proper paragraph separation — each paragraph is visually distinct with `
` line breaks between them.

Tables, code blocks, headings, and blockquotes all render correctly. Code blocks (`
`) preserve their internal `\n` formatting.

**Not tested:** `` with multi-line LaTeX in a live Telegram message (no math content in test conversation), but covered by unit test.

## AI-assisted

This PR was authored with AI assistance (OpenClaw agent). The agent:
1. Identified the bug by tracing the `markdownToTelegramRichHtml` → `renderMarkdownWithMarkers` → `escapeHtml` pipeline
2. Implemented the fix and iterated through 3 rounds of `codex review` / autoreview until clean
3. Ran the full telegram extension test suite (2160 tests)

I (Jet) reviewed each iteration and confirmed the fix resolves the paragraph break collapse in live Telegram messages.

## Checklist

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in PR description
- [x] Human-run real behavior proof included
- [x] `codex review --base origin/main` run locally, findings addressed
- [x] Tests pass locally
- [x] PR is focused (one fix, no unrelated changes)
- [x] No `CHANGELOG.md` changes

target.title: fix(dispatch): route durable reasoning payloads to Telegram
target.body:
## Summary

Under `/reasoning on`, Telegram users were not receiving thinking messages from reasoning models. The thinking content was correctly generated by the model and archived in the session, but the shared dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) unconditionally suppressed all `isReasoning` payloads before they could reach the Telegram-specific reasoning lane handler.

This PR routes durable `isReasoning` block and final payloads through to Telegram by skipping the generic suppression when the delivery channel is Telegram. Other channels (WhatsApp, web, etc.) continue to suppress reasoning payloads as before.

Fixes #94937

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `/reasoning on` was dropping all thinking messages on Telegram because the generic dispatch suppressed `isReasoning` payloads before Telegram's reasoning lane could process them.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v24.16.0, OpenClaw main @ 2026-06-19, test suite run via vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the full dispatch-from-config test suite, the Telegram bot-message-dispatch tests, and the reasoning emit tests to verify the fix and confirm no regressions on other channels.
```bash
# Run the dispatch-from-config tests (covers both block and final reply paths)
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts

# Run Telegram bot-message-dispatch tests (covers reasoning splitting/handling)
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts

# Run reasoning emit tests
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-reasoning-as-separate-message-enabled.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**: All 355 tests pass with 0 failures. The two-line change correctly gates the `isReasoning` suppression on the delivery channel — only skipping it for `telegram`.
```
✓ src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts (210 tests)
✓ extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts (129 tests)
✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-reasoning-as-separate-message-enabled.test.ts (16 tests)
```

The change is at two points in `dispatch-from-config.ts`:
- **Block delivery** (line ~3107): `payload.isReasoning === true` suppression now only applies when `deliveryChannel !== "telegram"`
- **Final reply loop** (line ~3279): `reply.isReasoning === true` skip now only applies when `deliveryChannel !== "telegram"`

**Observed result after the fix**: `isReasoning` block and final payloads now reach Telegram delivery, where the existing reasoning lane coordinator (`bot-message-dispatch.ts` lines 290-292, 932-934, 1164-1166) splits thinking from answer and persists them as separate messages under `/reasoning on`.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end Telegram session with a reasoning model (DeepSeek/Claude) using `/reasoning on`. Source-level coverage is provided by the 355 existing tests across dispatch, Telegram dispatch, and reasoning emit paths.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
# 210 passed

npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
# 129 passed

npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-reasoning-as-separate-message-enabled.test.ts
# 16 passed
```

### Integration / E2E

The change is a 2-line gate addition. Reasoning suppression behavior is unchanged for all non-Telegram channels. Telegram's existing reasoning lane handling (streaming split, progress draft, step state) is unchanged — the fix only ensures the payloads actually reach it.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — only affects Telegram; all other channels continue to suppress reasoning payloads exactly as before
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all 355 related tests pass
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation — no API/config changes to document
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — N/A, no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(workboard): filter archived cards in CLI list command
target.body:
## Summary

The `workboard_list` MCP tool and agent command filter out soft-archived cards by default. The CLI `workboard list` command did not apply the same filter, causing CLI/API inconsistency.

Add `.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt)` to the CLI list command to match the existing behavior in `command.ts` and `tools.ts`.

Fixes #94555

## What Problem This Solves

The `workboard list` CLI command displayed all cards including soft-archived cards (cards with `metadata.archivedAt` set). The `workboard_list` MCP tool (`tools.ts`) and the agent command (`command.ts`) already filter out archived cards by default using the same `.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt)` guard. Users saw inconsistent results between the CLI and MCP/agent interfaces, with archived cards cluttering the CLI output.

## Evidence

The CLI test suite passes with the added filter line. The guard is identical to the one already shipping in `command.ts` and `tools.ts`:

```bash
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/workboard/src/cli.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

The filter pattern at `cli.ts:136`:
```typescript
cards = cards.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt);
```

matches the identical pattern in:
- `command.ts:97`: `cards.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt)`
- `tools.ts:257`: `cards.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt)`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The `workboard list` CLI command displayed all cards including soft-archived ones (cards with `metadata.archivedAt` set). The `workboard_list` MCP tool (`tools.ts:257`) and the agent command (`command.ts:97`) already filter out archived cards by default using the same `.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt)` guard. This caused inconsistent behavior between CLI and MCP/agent interfaces.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node.js v24.13.1, Linux x86_64
- **Test framework**: Vitest v4.1.8
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-94555-workboard-list-filter`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
cd /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw
git checkout fix/issue-94555-workboard-list-filter
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/workboard/src/cli.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/workboard/src/cli.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

The added filter at `extensions/workboard/src/cli.ts:136`:
```typescript
// FIX #94555: Match the MCP tool / API behavior — exclude soft-archived
// cards by default unless explicitly opted in.
cards = cards.filter((card) => !card.metadata?.archivedAt);
```

is identical to the guard in `command.ts:97` and `tools.ts:257`.

**Observed result after the fix**: The CLI `workboard list` command now excludes soft-archived cards (those with `metadata.archivedAt` set), matching the behavior of the MCP `workboard_list` tool and the agent command. The fix is a single `.filter()` line that reuses the same guard pattern already present in two other code paths.

**What was not tested**: Live Workboard instance with real archived cards in a production environment. The filter logic is identical to two other shipping code paths (`command.ts` and `tools.ts`) that have been running in production without issues.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` — archived cards are no longer shown in CLI `workboard list` output, matching the MCP/API behavior. Users who relied on the CLI showing archived cards will need to use the explicit board view.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`.

**What is the highest-risk area?** Users who previously used CLI to view archived cards will no longer see them.

**How is that risk mitigated?** The MCP tool and agent command already filter out archived cards, so the MCP/API users already have this behavior. CLI users can still view all cards through the board-specific views.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested.

target.title: fix: recover Telegram channel after stop timeout in health monitor
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94008 — When a Telegram channel stop times out during an API outage, the health monitor could never recover the channel because it set `restartPending` but never actually cleaned up the stuck task state. This left the channel in `stopped` state permanently until a manual gateway restart.

## Root Cause

In `startChannelInternal`, when `recoveryStopTimedOut` was set and `store.tasks.has(id)` was true, the code added `recoveryStartRequested` and set `restartPending: true` then returned immediately without starting the channel. The start was supposed to happen when the stuck stop eventually completed but if the stop never completed (channel torn down), the callback was never triggered and the channel stayed dead.

## Fix

When the health monitor retries recovery (i.e. `recoveryStartRequested` is already set), clean up the stuck task state and allow the channel to start normally instead of deferring indefinitely.

## Testing

- `src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.test.ts` — 136 tests passed
- Lint: clean (oxlint)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: After Telegram API outage, health monitor permanently fails to restart channel stuck in stopped state.

**Real environment tested**: Windows 10 LTSC 2019, Node.js v24.14.0, OpenClaw worktree on main with fix applied.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
npx oxlint --config .oxlintrc.json src/gateway/server-channels.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
Lint: no issues found
channel-health-monitor.test.ts: 136 tests passed (4 test files)
```

**Observed result after fix**: When health monitor retries recovery after a stuck stop, the stale task state is cleaned up allowing a fresh start.

**What was not tested**: Live Telegram API outage scenario (no Telegram bot available).

target.title: fix(voice-call): add onBlockReply to runEmbeddedAgent for early TTS
target.body:
## Root Cause

When `generateVoiceResponse()` calls `agentRuntime.runEmbeddedAgent()`, it relies on `result.payloads` to extract spoken text via `extractSpokenTextFromPayloads()`. However, the embedded agent's post-turn compaction can complete *after* payloads are streamed, causing `result.payloads` to be empty or stale by the time TTS reads them. This means the voice call hears silence or truncated speech even though the agent produced a response.

## Summary

- Voice call TTS (text-to-speech) was receiving empty or stale payloads because `runEmbeddedAgent()` post-turn compaction could finish after the payloads were already streamed, and only `result.payloads` (the post-compaction aggregate) was consulted.
- This PR adds an `onBlockReply` callback (with `blockReplyBreak: "text_end"`) to `runEmbeddedAgent()` in `generateVoiceResponse()`, matching the same pattern already used by Telegram, Matrix, Copilot, and Codex channels to collect payloads before compaction completes. If `onBlockReply` payloads are available, they are preferred over `result.payloads`.
- Fixes #79521

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/voice-call` — 50 test files, 457 tests passed
- `pnpm build` — successful
- Autoreview: clean (2 non-blocking findings: P2 test gap for onBlockReply data collection path, P3 test type imprecision)
- CI: `check-test-types` fixed — added mock parameter type for `onBlockReply` in `createAgentRuntime`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Voice call TTS was receiving empty spoken text because `runEmbeddedAgent()` post-turn compaction could finish after streaming, making `result.payloads` empty when read.

Real environment tested: Linux (Node 22), branch `fix/issue-79521-voice-call-block-reply`, commit `d875bb8114`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test extensions/voice-call
pnpm build
pnpm tsgo:test
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ pnpm test extensions/voice-call

✓ extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts (10 tests) 49ms
  ✓ generateVoiceResponse (10 tests) 49ms
    ✓ suppresses reasoning payloads and reads structured spoken output
    ✓ extracts spoken text from fenced JSON
    ✓ returns silence for an explicit empty spoken contract response
    ✓ strips leading planning text when model returns plain text
    ✓ keeps plain conversational output when no JSON contract is followed
    ✓ pins the voice session to responseModel before running the embedded agent
    ✓ uses the persisted per-call session key for classic responses
    ✓ uses the main agent workspace when voice config omits agentId
    ✓ uses the configured voice response agent workspace
    ✓ passes the routed voice agent explicit tool allowlist to the embedded run

Test Files  50 passed (50)
     Tests  457 passed (457)
      Time  22.087s (in thread 4.271s, 517.38%)

$ pnpm build
> openclaw@2026.6.5 build /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw
> turbo run build --filter='!docs' --filter='!@openclaw/apple-neural-engine'
...
Tasks: 79 successful, 79 total

$ pnpm tsgo:test
> pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
> node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json ...
> node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json ...
(no type errors — previously failing line 192 now passes)
```

Observed result after fix: All 10 voice-call response generator tests pass, including new assertions that `blockReplyBreak` is `"text_end"` and `onBlockReply` is a function. Type check (`tsgo:test`) now passes cleanly — the previously failing `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.test.ts:192` type errors (TS2493, TS2339) are resolved by adding parameter type annotation to the `runEmbeddedAgent` mock. Build completes successfully (79 tasks). The fix follows the established `blockReplyBreak`/`onBlockReply` contract already proven in Telegram, Matrix, Copilot, and Codex channels.

What was not tested: The `onBlockReply` data collection path is exercised only indirectly via the type assertion (`typeof ... onBlockReply` is `"function"`). A unit test that verifies payloads collected via `onBlockReply` are preferred over `result.payloads` is a follow-up item (tracked as P2 in review). No live Twilio/VoIP roundtrip was performed.

target.title: feat(i18n): add Hebrew (he) locale
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw had no Hebrew (`he`) locale. Hebrew users got the English Control UI and no Hebrew docs.

## Why This Change Was Made

Registers `he` end-to-end so the existing automated translation pipeline (Control UI locale refresh + docs `translate-all`) generates and maintains Hebrew like every other locale. Contributors only prepare the locale; org-side automation does the translation.

## User Impact

- Hebrew appears in the Control UI language switcher and loads as a lazy locale bundle.
- Browser language `he` (and legacy `iw`) auto-resolves to Hebrew.
- RTL renders automatically (existing `detectTextDirection` already handles the Hebrew script).
- Docs translation will produce Hebrew pages once `he` is added to the publish-repo matrix (`openclaw/docs`).

## Evidence

- Wiring: `ui/src/i18n/lib/types.ts`, `registry.ts` (lazy loader + `resolveNavigatorLocale`), `scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts`, `control-ui-i18n-report.ts`, language label in `en.ts`.
- Pipeline: `he` added to the `control-ui-locale-refresh.yml` matrix so CI auto-translates with the org key; placeholder `he.ts` generated via the standard generator (English fallback, marked pending).
- Docs: `docs/.i18n/glossary.he.json` + `he-navigation.json`.
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check` → `he: clean` (all locales clean).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts` → 11/11 passing (`he` added to structural parity coverage).
- `pnpm --dir ui build` → succeeds; emits separate `he-*.js` chunk.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(gateway): surface cross-agent subagent sessions via allAgents flag (#95295)
target.body:
# fix(gateway): surface cross-agent subagent sessions via configuredAgentsOnly + no agentId (#95295)

Fixes #95295 — Control UI's left "Recent Sessions" sidebar hid child-spawned subagent sessions whose owning agent differed from the viewer's scope. Multi-agent workflows (e.g. main spawning orchestrator/reviewer subagents) lost visibility from any non-owning agent view.

## Summary

Surface cross-agent child-spawned subagent sessions in the Control UI Sessions table and left Recent Sessions sidebar **without adding a new public `sessions.list` protocol field**. The cross-agent visibility path is the existing `configuredAgentsOnly: true` + no `agentId` shape:

- The combined session-store loader already pulls every configured agent's store when `configuredAgentsOnly: true` is set and `agentId` is omitted.
- `filterSessionEntries` skips its strict per-agent match naturally when `agentId` is unset, so child-spawned subagent sessions owned by peer agents surface.
- The Control UI's **All agents** toggle now controls *only* the sidebar projection filter (`resolveSidebarRecentSessions`) and triggers a plain `loadSessions(state)` reload. No new request field is sent.
- Explicit `agentId` overrides (e.g. `ensureSkillWorkshopRevisionSessionsLoaded`) are **always** honored regardless of toggle state — scoped refreshes never silently widen.

## Behavior

- **Toggle off (default):** sidebar shows sessions for the currently selected agent only (no subagent rows, no cross-agent rows). Session table shows sessions for the selected agent.
- **Toggle on:** sidebar surfaces cross-agent child-spawned subagent sessions alongside the viewer's own (sidebar top-5 by updatedAt desc). Session table shows every configured agent's sessions. Archived / global / unknown / cron rows stay filtered either way.
- **Explicit scoped refresh** (`loadSessions(state, { agentId: "x" }`) is always respected, even when the toggle is on.

## Why no new `allAgents` flag

ClawSweeper review flagged the previous `allAgents` additive protocol field as duplicating the existing `configuredAgentsOnly + no agentId` shape. This commit removes the duplicate. The Swift `SessionsListParams` mirror drops `allagents`; existing OpenClawKit callers stay source-compatible because the Swift mirror already defaulted `agentId: String? = nil`.

## Repro (from issue)

User-reported numbers: 5 of 332 subagent sessions were visible to the Control UI. This restores the remaining 327 by expressing the cross-agent visibility path via the existing protocol shape. From #95295:

> Web UI sidebar shows only 5 sessions (main/disk/recovered types), zero child-spawned sessions
> `filterSessionEntries()` in session-utils-CHNCuY8a.js filters by `agentId` when Web UI calls `sessions.list`, excluding cross-agent child-spawned sessions

## Real-world validation

End-to-end behavior verified against a live `pnpm openclaw --dev gateway` instance with two configured agents (`dev`, `orchestrator`). Three real `sessions_spawn` tool calls from `agent:dev:main` produced the cross-agent session entries below — not seeded store files.

Spawned subagents (entries written by real `sessions_spawn` tool calls, all `spawnedBy: agent:dev:main`):

| Label | Session key | Run id |
|---|---|---|
| Review PR #95295 cross-agent visibility | `agent:orchestrator:subagent:4a1dbaa0-cf6f-4001-bdf6-aa2dd518a1a3` | `3700fc94-231d-4dbf-85c0-7e5b8f70d7ee` |
| Reproduce 5 of 332 sidebar ratio | `agent:orchestrator:subagent:0e857222-2fad-4df4-a5bd-8941a4524867` | `00ef5855-86a9-433e-96f2-afae35f12042` |
| Verify allAgents filter toggle | `agent:orchestrator:subagent:dd4d23ba-21b1-40fe-b957-289531108fdd` | `74ac38db-dc49-4cd9-a0ce-aa65ef39274c` |

The subagent runs themselves failed at model step (gpt-5.5 OpenAI key 401 in this dev env), but the cross-agent session entries are persisted in the orchestrator store — exactly the surface this fix makes visible.

**Before fix** (orchestrator store empty, baseline): sidebar shows **1 row** — only `agent:dev:main`. Cross-agent subagent sessions are invisible.

![Sidebar before fix — 1 row](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/NianJiuZst/1130b0d32e4bdbefb777b91e46e0f58c/raw/ed9a2bfaac30f59cbc39570ffc4f87eebb2a7a0e/sidebar-before-fix.png)

**After fix** (3 real cross-agent sessions in store, **All agents** toggle on): sidebar shows **4 rows** — `agent:dev:main` + the 3 `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` sessions above. The fix surfaces them.

![Sidebar after fix — 4 rows with cross-agent subagent sessions](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/NianJiuZst/1130b0d32e4bdbefb777b91e46e0f58c/raw/4a40a6daf61c75588da1e5b924b20312cb6d800b/sidebar-after-fix.png)

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.sidebar.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils` — all pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — green.
- `pnpm oxfmt --check` on touched files — clean.
- `pnpm protocol:gen:swift` regenerates `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift` to a byte-identical mirror of the manual edit.
- `git diff --check` — no conflict markers.

## Touched surfaces

- `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/sessions.ts` — drop `allAgents` from `SessionsListParamsSchema` (kept on `SessionsCleanupParamsSchema`, independent feature)
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift` — regenerated mirror; `SessionsListParams` no longer carries `allagents`
- `scripts/protocol-gen-swift.ts` — drop `allAgents` from `SessionsListParams` nil-default mapping
- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` — `filterSessionEntries` opts out of `agentId` strict match naturally when `agentId` is unset; cross-agent rows pass through.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` — handler correctly forwards `configuredAgentsOnly` to `loadCombinedSessionStoreForGateway` (previous code computed it but did not pass it, masking the test's intent); explicit `agentId` overrides always respected.
- `src/tui/embedded-backend.ts` — TUI `listSessions` mirrors the server semantics; drops the `allAgents` branch.
- `ui/src/ui/app.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` — `sessionsAllAgents` UI state retained as the **sidebar projection** flag; no longer leaks to the protocol.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts` — `loadSessions` no longer forwards `allAgents`; explicit `agentId` overrides are always honored.
- `ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts` — **All agents** toggle label retained (UI-only).
- `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` — `sessionsView.allAgents` / `allAgentsTooltip` retained.
- `src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts` — cross-agent test uses the canonical `configuredAgentsOnly + no agentId` shape; `configuredAgentsOnly hides unrelated stores` test reflects the now-correctly-forwarded loader behavior.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` — rewritten to cover the new shape: explicit override always scopes; no override queries every configured agent.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.sidebar.test.ts` — sidebar projection regression test (hide-by-default, surface-when-toggle-on, keep-archived-rows-hidden).

## Release note

Surfacing cross-agent child-spawned subagent sessions in the Control UI "Recent Sessions" sidebar and Sessions table when the **All agents** toggle is enabled. Default single-agent behavior is unchanged. No new public protocol field added — cross-agent visibility is expressed via the existing `configuredAgentsOnly: true` + no `agentId` request shape. Explicit per-agent refresh overrides are always honored.

Refs #95295. Reported-by: rweichitsai

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  84 passed (84)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  36 passed (36)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.16s

  $ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
  Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

## Current head addendum: `8c164eeaa3`

ClawSweeper's `a12e1bbcf0` review finding is addressed in this head:

- Family target selection still reserves the current active transcript before the 32-target cap is reached.
- Family output order is now ancestor transcripts first, then current-session reset archives, then the current active transcript, so final `limit` trimming keeps the active session tail.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` mirrors the Gateway order.
- Added over-limit regressions proving `includeFamily` with a small final limit keeps current active history.

Standalone redacted embedded `chat.history` smoke from current head `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Creates a temporary session.store, one ancestor transcript, and one current transcript;
# calls createEmbeddedCallGateway().chat.history with default, includeFamily, and includeFamily+limit.
TS
{
  "default": [
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamily": [
    "redacted ancestor context",
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamilyLimit1": [
    "redacted current context"
  ]
}
```

Validation on `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

# passed

## Current head addendum: `0d37b42702`

Maintainer readiness note addressed on current head `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

- Removed the `resetAncestors` sessions_history alias so the model-facing selector is only `includeFamily`.
- Kept Gateway `chat.history` and `sessions_history` aligned on the single `includeFamily` flag.

target.title: Speed up precomputed command help startup
target.body:
## Summary
- add strict launcher/entry fast paths for precomputed `doctor`, `gateway`, `models`, `plugins`, `sessions`, and `tasks` help
- keep container routing in control for those precomputed subcommand help paths
- fix Windows `node --import` RSS hook paths in startup benchmark tooling

AI-assisted: yes. I understand what this code does: it consumes existing precomputed subcommand help metadata earlier in the launcher/entry startup path, while preserving runtime fallthrough for container-sensitive help.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor --help`, `openclaw gateway --help`, `openclaw models --help`, `openclaw plugins --help`, `openclaw sessions --help`, and `openclaw tasks --help` now return precomputed help from the launcher/entry fast path instead of importing the full runtime before printing help.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 local OpenClaw checkout in `C:\Users\Yeqiu\Documents\agent-windows-lab\worktrees\openclaw-perf`, Node v24.9.0, pnpm toolchain from the repo, running the built CLI benchmark against the patched branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```powershell
node --import tsx scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case doctorHelp --case gatewayHelp --case modelsHelp --case pluginsHelp --runs 3 --warmup 1 --timeout-ms 10000 --output .artifacts/openclaw-startup-final-after-review.json
node --import tsx scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case sessionsHelp --case tasksHelp --runs 5 --warmup 1 --json --output .artifacts\cli-startup-more-final-after-entry.json
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from benchmark runs:
```text
doctor --help: avg=50.5ms firstOutputAvg=42.1ms rssAvg=46.1MB code=0x3
gateway --help: avg=59.1ms firstOutputAvg=49.7ms rssAvg=46.2MB code=0x3
models --help: avg=61.5ms firstOutputAvg=51.9ms rssAvg=46.3MB code=0x3
plugins --help: avg=55.5ms firstOutputAvg=46.5ms rssAvg=46.3MB code=0x3
sessions --help: avg=56.4ms firstOutputAvg=47.5ms rssAvg=47.4MB code=0x5
tasks --help: avg=53.0ms firstOutputAvg=44.8ms rssAvg=47.5MB code=0x5
```

Observed result after fix: All six changed direct subcommand help invocations exited successfully and printed first output in about 42-52 ms for the first four commands and about 45-48 ms for `sessions`/`tasks`. Earlier local fallback measurements for `sessions --help` and `tasks --help` were about 1.6-1.8s with about 253-256 MB RSS, so these help-only invocations now avoid the full runtime import.

What was not tested: Full cross-platform CI matrix locally. Focused launcher/runtime behavior, container fallthrough, startup benchmark tooling, and the copied live benchmark output above were tested locally. GitHub CI is rerunning after the latest push.

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write openclaw.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts src/entry.ts src/entry.test.ts`

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: feat(cli): add --message-file to openclaw agent
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw agent` currently requires inline `--message`, which makes long prompts, Markdown/code blocks, JSON, and Windows PowerShell multiline content awkward or fragile to pass through shell quoting.
- This PR adds `--message-file <path>` to the `openclaw agent` one-shot CLI, reads the file as valid UTF-8, strips an optional UTF-8 BOM, and preserves multiline content for both Gateway and `--local` dispatch.
- Inline `--message` behavior stays unchanged: it is still trimmed and sent through the existing dispatch path.
- Out of scope: `openclaw message send`, channel adapter chunking/truncation semantics, live delivery limits, and any transport/protocol changes.
- Success looks like callers being able to run `openclaw agent --agent ops --message-file ./task.md` without shell-escaping the prompt, while clear errors catch missing files, invalid UTF-8, empty files, or passing both message inputs.
- Reviewer focus: CLI option semantics, whether strict UTF-8 rejection is the right contract, and preserving the exact file body after the new input boundary.

## Linked context

No separate issue. This is a small CLI ergonomics gap found while trying to hand off multiline agent work from Windows/PowerShell.

Related #79182 and #79200 cover the adjacent `openclaw message send --message-file` surface. This PR intentionally targets `openclaw agent` only and does not close that issue or replace that PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent` can now accept a multiline prompt from `--message-file` instead of requiring all content to fit safely inside a shell-quoted `--message` argument.
- Real environment tested: Windows source checkout on branch `fix/agent-message-file`, commit `7614ed4e36`, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, built source checkout, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` under `.artifacts/agent-message-file-proof`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
# task.md contained:
# first line
# ```json
# {"ok":true}
# ```
# second line

node scripts/run-node.mjs --help

node openclaw.mjs agent `
  --message-file .artifacts/agent-message-file-proof/task.md `
  --json

node openclaw.mjs agent `
  --message inline `
  --message-file .artifacts/agent-message-file-proof/task.md `
  --agent main `
  --json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
STDOUT:

STDERR:
Error: No target session selected. Use --agent <id>, --session-key <key>, --session-id <id>, or --to <E.164>. Run openclaw agents list to see agents.

EXIT=1
```

```text
STDOUT:

STDERR:
Error: Use either --message or --message-file, not both.

EXIT=1
```

- Observed result after fix: the first command accepts `--message-file`, reads the file, and advances past message validation to the next real validation boundary (`No target session selected`). Before this patch, Commander required `--message` and would not accept a file-only agent invocation. The second command confirms the new input is mutually exclusive with inline `--message`.
- What was not tested: live Gateway/model execution or channel delivery with a real configured agent. Exact Gateway parameter body preservation, missing-file handling, and invalid UTF-8 rejection are covered by focused tests below.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the real CLI smoke intentionally stops before Gateway dispatch by omitting a target selector, so it proves the changed CLI input boundary without requiring local credentials, a running Gateway, or a model call.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection before the patch showed `openclaw agent` registered `.requiredOption("-m, --message <text>", ...)`, so `--message-file` could not be used as the sole message input.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```powershell
pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent-turn.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts docs/cli/agent.md docs/tools/agent-send.md
pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent-turn.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/agent.md docs/tools/agent-send.md
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
oxfmt: passed
oxlint: passed
register.agent.test.ts: 16 passed
agent-via-gateway.test.ts: 60 passed
tsgo:core: passed
tsgo:core:test: passed
Docs MDX check passed (2 files, 47ms)
git diff --check: passed
```

Regression coverage added:

- CLI parser forwards `--message-file` to the agent command without requiring inline `--message`.
- Gateway dispatch reads a UTF-8 file, removes an optional BOM, and preserves multiline content including code fences and the trailing newline.
- Passing both `--message` and `--message-file` fails before dispatch.
- Missing message files fail before dispatch with `Message file not found`.
- Invalid UTF-8 files fail before dispatch with `Message file must be valid UTF-8`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `openclaw agent` now accepts `--message-file <path>` as an alternative to inline `--message`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The CLI reads only the caller-supplied local path under the current OS user, before existing Gateway/local dispatch.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prompt content boundaries: accidentally trimming or corrupting file content before dispatch, or silently accepting non-UTF-8 input.

How is that risk mitigated?

Inline messages keep the old trim behavior, file messages preserve content after BOM removal, strict UTF-8 decoding rejects corrupt input, and focused tests assert the exact Gateway `params.message` value plus error paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub CI, ClawSweeper, and maintainer review after PR creation.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and ClawSweeper are pending. No known author-side blocker remains.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet. This is a new PR.

target.title: fix(web-ui): skip hidden subagent picker pages
target.body:
Fixes #89249.

Summary:
- Advance the chat session picker through raw `sessions.list` pages that add no visible rows after hidden spawn-child subagent filtering.
- Stop showing raw total counts when the picker is filtering out loaded rows, avoiding misleading states like `1 / 177`.
- Add browser E2E regression coverage for a hidden-only page followed by a visible session page.

Surface area:
- No public config schema/options changes.
- No plugin API/contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surface.

Validation:
- `OPENCLAW_CAPTURE_UI_PROOF=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 passed; screenshot proof captured at `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` (1280x900 PNG).
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat session picker" --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 6 passed, 89 skipped.
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> 0 warnings/errors.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` -> passed.

Real behavior proof:
- Added a Playwright/Chromium Control UI E2E test that drives the actual chat picker in a browser against the mocked Gateway Control UI server.
- The test creates a visible first page plus a hidden subagent row, then clicks Load More. The mocked backend returns a hidden-only raw page at offset `2`; the picker automatically requests offset `4` and renders the next visible top-level chat.
- Assertions cover the requested offsets (`2`, then `4`), the visible picker count (`2`), absence of hidden subagent options, and removal of the Load More button after all visible rows are loaded.
- Proof environment: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Playwright Chromium, local `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` screenshot from the passing browser run.

Signed head:
- `c6b0342f48c4a67d475d40e35edd40110a95cbcc` (`gpgsig` present)
